MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth C. Sears, Esq., of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party The Regents of the University of California
Tarlan Banazadeh, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Debra Witt

RULING:
DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

The Court disclosed its relationship with Charles Robinson, General Counsel of The Regents of the University of California. No objection was made.
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Regents of the University of California's motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. George was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony and declaration are overruled. Govt. Code 835 was not the only theory of liability alleged against defendant in plaintiff's amended complaint. To the extent that defendant made an alternative motion for summary adjudication, the motion is denied due to lack of compliance with summary adjudication rules requiring that each issue sought to be summarily adjudicated be separately identified and supported with specified purported undisputed facts. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542 = (220/HEK)
